content_markdown: Two pipes have the same length. Pipe X is open at both ends while pipe Y is closed at one end. What is the ratio of the fundamental frequency in pipe X to that in pipe Y?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: For a pipe of length $L$ open at both ends (Pipe X), the fundamental wavelength is $\lambda_X = 2L$, so the frequency is $f_X = \frac{v}{2L}$. For a pipe closed at one end (Pipe Y), the fundamental wavelength is $\lambda_Y = 4L$, so the frequency is $f_Y = \frac{v}{4L}$. The ratio $\frac{f_X}{f_Y} = \frac{v/2L}{v/4L} = \frac{4}{2} = 2$.
